stories_covered: Cursor acquired by XAI/SpaceX for $60 billion with $10 billion break clause
Tim Cook stepping down from Apple after 15-year run
Anthropic crosses $1 trillion valuation in secondary markets, rejects $800 billion new funding
Anthropic launches Claude Design, competing directly with Figma and Adobe
harry_takes: Cursor acquisition is the highest private M&A exit ever at $60 billion, achieved in just three years
Elon gets enormous cost-of-capital arbitrage: SpaceX trades at 100X revenue, can buy 10-15X revenue assets 'all day long'
Anthropic's Claude Design poses an existential threat not to Figma directly but by enabling normal people to bypass designers entirely
There will be a $100 billion deal in the next 12 months as mega-cap tech companies scramble to lock down AI capabilities
Tim Cook's exit is operationally excellent—barely moved the stock—signaling perfect succession management
harry_boldest_quote: This is the biggest private acquisition ever in venture, period, full stop, end of story.
predictions: Harry: There will be a $100 billion deal in the next 12 months among the mega-cap acquirers
Harry: This Cursor deal will stand as the high water mark of private M&A for a decade
Rory: Claude Design isn't a direct Figma killer but will maim Figma over time as non-designers build on their own
disagreements: Harry predicted a $100 billion deal coming; Rory pushed back on math that only Elon can write such checks, but agreed smaller deals ($10-20B) will accelerate due to expanded Overton window.
harry_role: Driving both narrative and debate, articulating the Elon arbitrage thesis and the Anthropic moat-expansion story, challenging Jason on competitive threats to incumbents.
notable: Jason's point that Elon's ability to write checks at 100X revenue multiples creates a self-reinforcing advantage loop that no other founder can replicate becomes the underlying theme of deal-making in 2026.
